title: rubus × hollandrei f. subprocerus
intro: Rubus × hollandrei f. subprocerus, also known as subprocerus blackberry, is a hybrid plant species within the Rubus genus, characterized by its thorny stems and dark purple fruit. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, as well as protecting against cell damage and oxidative stress. Subprocerus blackberry can be prepared using various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and immune function. Additionally, the plant's leaves and roots can be used to make salves and ointments for topical applications, such as wound healing and skin protection.
